candidates.action_name: Genetic counseling and cascade testing for ATM pathogenic variant
candidates.trigger_summary: ATM c.8147T>C heterozygous pathogenic variant documented.
candidates.guideline_anchor: ACMG Secondary Findings v3.2 / NCCN Genetic-Familial High-Risk Assessment
candidates.intervention: Refer to cancer genetics for variant counseling, gene-specific surveillance review, and cascade testing offer to adult first-degree relatives.
candidates.tracking_metric: Genetics visit completed; cascade offer documented for adult first-degree relatives.
candidates.rationale: A pathogenic ATM finding is a heritable cancer-risk result with implications for the patient and relatives. The standard action is counseling and cascade testing, not LLM ranking.
candidates.expected_query: ACMG SF v3.2 ATM secondary findings cascade testing NCCN genetic familial high risk

citations.title: InSiGHT 2022 Abstract Publishing and Best Abstract Awards

candidates.biomcp_query: ACMG SF v3.2 ATM secondary findings cascade testing NCCN genetic familial high risk

biomcp_results.title: InSiGHT 2022 Abstract Publishing and Best Abstract Awards

candidates.action_name: Baseline PSA discussion and test for ATM carrier
candidates.trigger_summary: Male, age 47, ATM pathogenic variant; no PSA value documented.
candidates.guideline_anchor: NCCN Prostate Cancer Early Detection high-risk germline pathway / Philadelphia consensus
candidates.intervention: Shared-decision discussion and baseline serum PSA now; set follow-up cadence from result and high-risk pathway.
candidates.tracking_metric: PSA value documented; repeat interval or urology threshold recorded.
candidates.rationale: High-risk germline prostate pathways begin earlier than population-average PSA discussions. This is a true missed SoC candidate in the prior run.
candidates.expected_query: ATM germline mutation prostate cancer screening PSA NCCN Philadelphia consensus

Prostate Cancer: genetics in practice now and in the future.

Prostate cancer genetic background. The introduction of genetic testing in the determination of high-risk prostate cancer cases and selection of targeted chemotherapy in advanced prostate cancer patients.

candidates.action_name: Clarify paternal GI cancer primary site before pancreatic-surveillance pin
candidates.trigger_summary: Family history says paternal grandfather “GI cancer” but does not document pancreatic primary.
candidates.guideline_anchor: NCCN/CAPS high-risk pancreatic surveillance requires mutation-specific family-history details
candidates.intervention: Obtain structured three-generation family history: cancer primary site, age at diagnosis, lineage, and pathology if available.
candidates.tracking_metric: Family-history primary site documented as pancreatic vs non-pancreatic GI; pancreas surveillance eligibility recomputed.
candidates.rationale: The prior prototype pinned pancreatic surveillance from an ambiguous GI-cancer note. The real method should not convert unspecified GI cancer into PDAC eligibility without confirmation.
candidates.expected_query: CAPS consortium pancreatic surveillance ATM family history criteria

candidates.trigger_summary: Age 47; no prior colorectal screening documented.
candidates.guideline_anchor: USPSTF colorectal cancer screening adults 45–75, Grade A/B
candidates.intervention: Complete colorectal cancer screening now. Colonoscopy is preferred if family-history clarification may change interval; stool-based options acceptable if average-risk pathway chosen.
candidates.tracking_metric: CRC screening completed; modality and next interval documented.
candidates.rationale: Age alone triggers CRC screening. ATM may affect risk discussion, but the guideline-safe trigger is universal age 45+ screening unless confirmed syndrome-specific criteria apply.

candidates.action_name: Hepatitis B vaccination status check and completion
candidates.trigger_summary: Age 47; hepatitis B vaccination or immunity not documented.
candidates.guideline_anchor: CDC/ACIP universal hepatitis B vaccination adults 19–59
candidates.intervention: Verify HepB vaccine series or immunity; vaccinate if incomplete/nonimmune.
candidates.rationale: Universal adult immunization rule; not a risk-model insight, but true SoC.
candidates.expected_query: CDC ACIP hepatitis B vaccination adults 19 59 universal recommendation

candidates.action_name: Confirm kidney status with cystatin C eGFR and urine ACR
candidates.trigger_summary: eGFRcr 72 with creatinine 1.25; cystatin C and urine ACR not documented.
candidates.guideline_anchor: KDIGO 2024 CKD evaluation: confirm GFR when creatinine may be biased; assess albuminuria
candidates.intervention: Order serum cystatin C for eGFRcr-cys and urine albumin-creatinine ratio.
candidates.tracking_metric: eGFRcr-cys and UACR documented; CKD category confirmed or removed.
candidates.rationale: Creatinine-based eGFR can be biased by muscle mass. UACR is required to stage kidney risk; cystatin C resolves whether eGFRcr 72 is true G2 or phenotype artifact.
candidates.expected_query: KDIGO 2024 cystatin C creatinine muscle mass urine albumin creatinine ratio CKD evaluation

considered_and_excluded.reason: Not automatically eligible: age 47, CAC=0, normotensive, A1c 5.3, Lp(a) 72 nmol/L optimal, estimated 10-year ASCVD ~low. Revisit if risk estimate/risk enhancers change.

considered_and_excluded.action_name: hs-CRP as a standalone SoC action
considered_and_excluded.reason: ACC/AHA uses hs-CRP as a risk-enhancer when statin decision is uncertain in borderline/intermediate risk. Current CAC=0 and very-low estimated risk make EVOI low; can be bundled with future lipids but should not rank above true universal gaps.
Not pinned from current source data because family history documents “GI cancer,” not confirmed pancreatic cancer. Needs family-history clarification first.
